title: [Inliner] Keep nonnull information around when inlining
author: philnik777

tests.test_body:
; This test runs the inliner and the function attribute deduction. It ensures
; that when the inliner mutates the call graph it correctly updates the CGSCC
; iteration so that we can compute refined function attributes. In this way it
; is leveraging function attribute computation to observe correct call graph
; updates.

; Boring unknown external function call.
declare void @unknown()

; Basic correctness check: this should get annotated as memory(none).
declare void @readnone() readnone nounwind

; The 'test1_' prefixed functions are designed to trigger forming a new direct
; call in the inlined body of the function. After that, we form a new SCC and
; using that can deduce precise function attrs.

; This function should no longer exist.
define internal void @test1_f(ptr %p) {
entry:
  call void %p()
  ret void
}

; This function should have had 'memory(none)' deduced for its SCC.
define void @test1_g() noinline {
entry:
  call void @test1_f(ptr @test1_h)
  ret void
}

; This function should have had 'memory(none)' deduced for its SCC.
define void @test1_h() noinline {
entry:
  call void @test1_g()
  call void @readnone()
  ret void
}


; The 'test2_' prefixed functions are designed to trigger forming a new direct
; call due to RAUW-ing the returned value of a called function into the caller.
; This too should form a new SCC which can then be reasoned about to compute
; precise function attrs.

; This function should no longer exist.
define internal ptr @test2_f() {
entry:
  ret ptr @test2_h
}

; This function should have had 'memory(none)' deduced for its SCC.
define void @test2_g() noinline {
entry:
  %p = call ptr @test2_f()
  call void %p()
  ret void
}

; This function should have had 'memory(none)' deduced for its SCC.
define void @test2_h() noinline {
entry:
  call void @test2_g()
  call void @readnone()
  ret void
}


; The 'test3_' prefixed functions are designed to inline in a way that causes
; call sites to become trivially dead during the middle of inlining callsites of
; a single function to make sure that the inliner does not get confused by this
; pattern.

define internal void @test3_maybe_unknown(i1 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %b, label %then, label %exit

then:
  call void @unknown()
  br label %exit

exit:
  ret void
}

define internal i1 @test3_f() {
entry:
  ret i1 false
}

define internal i1 @test3_g(i1 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %b, label %then1, label %if2

then1:
  call void @test3_maybe_unknown(i1 true)
  br label %if2

if2:
  %f = call i1 @test3_f()
  br i1 %f, label %then2, label %exit

then2:
  call void @test3_maybe_unknown(i1 true)
  br label %exit

exit:
  ret i1 false
}

; FIXME: Currently the inliner doesn't successfully mark this as readnone
; because while it simplifies trivially dead CFGs when inlining callees it
; doesn't simplify the caller's trivially dead CFG and so we end with a dead
; block calling @unknown.
define void @test3_h() {
entry:
  %g = call i1 @test3_g(i1 false)
  br i1 %g, label %then, label %exit

then:
  call void @test3_maybe_unknown(i1 true)
  br label %exit

exit:
  call void @test3_maybe_unknown(i1 false)
  ret void
}


; The 'test4_' prefixed functions are designed to trigger forming a new direct
; call in the inlined body of the function similar to 'test1_'. However, after
; that we continue to inline another edge of the graph forcing us to do a more
; interesting call graph update for the new call edge. Eventually, we still
; form a new SCC and should use that can deduce precise function attrs.

; This function should have had 'memory(none)' deduced for its SCC.
define void @test4_f1() noinline {
entry:
  call void @test4_h()
  ret void
}

define internal void @test4_f2() {
entry:
  call void @test4_f1()
  ret void
}

define internal void @test4_g(ptr %p) {
entry:
  call void %p()
  ret void
}

; This function should have had 'memory(none)' deduced for its SCC.
define void @test4_h() noinline {
entry:
  call void @test4_g(ptr @test4_f2)
  ret void
}

declare ptr @buz()

declare i32 @__gxx_personality_v0(...)

declare ptr @simple_func(ptr)

define ptr @on_invoke() personality ptr @__gxx_personality_v0 {
  %r = call ptr @buz()
  %1 = invoke nonnull ptr @simple_func(ptr %r)
          to label %proceed unwind label %pad

proceed:                                          ; preds = %0
  ret ptr %r

pad:                                              ; preds = %0
  %2 = landingpad { ptr, i32 }
          cleanup
  ret ptr %r
}

; Function Attrs: alwaysinline
declare hidden ptr @callee_with_throwable(ptr) #0

; Function Attrs: alwaysinline
declare hidden ptr @callee_with_explicit_control_flow(ptr) #0

define ptr @caller2(ptr %ptr, i64 %x, i1 %cond) {
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %x
  %p = call nonnull ptr @callee_with_throwable(ptr %gep)
  %q = call nonnull ptr @callee_with_explicit_control_flow(ptr %gep)
  br i1 %cond, label %pret, label %qret

pret:                                             ; preds = %0
  ret ptr %p

qret:                                             ; preds = %0
  ret ptr %q
}

attributes #0 = { alwaysinline }

target datalayout = "e-m:o-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"
target triple = "arm64-apple-macosx11.0.0"

%"class.std::__1::span" = type { ptr, i64 }

define dso_local noundef i32 @sum_prefix_with_sum(ptr %s.coerce0, i64 %s.coerce1, i64 noundef %n) {
entry:
  %s = alloca %"class.std::__1::span", align 8
  %n.addr = alloca i64, align 8
  %ret = alloca i32, align 4
  %i = alloca i64, align 8
  %0 = getelementptr inbounds { ptr, i64 }, ptr %s, i32 0, i32 0
  store ptr %s.coerce0, ptr %0, align 8
  %1 = getelementptr inbounds { ptr, i64 }, ptr %s, i32 0, i32 1
  store i64 %s.coerce1, ptr %1, align 8
  store i64 %n, ptr %n.addr, align 8
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %ret)
  store i32 0, ptr %ret, align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %i)
  store i64 0, ptr %i, align 8
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %2 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %3 = load i64, ptr %n.addr, align 8
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %2, %3
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %i)
  br label %for.end

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  %4 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %call = call noundef nonnull align 4 dereferenceable(4) ptr @span_checked_access(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(16) %s, i64 noundef %4)
  %5 = load i32, ptr %call, align 4
  %6 = load i32, ptr %ret, align 4
  %add = add nsw i32 %6, %5
  store i32 %add, ptr %ret, align 4
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  %7 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %inc = add i64 %7, 1
  store i64 %inc, ptr %i, align 8
  br label %for.cond

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup
  %8 = load i32, ptr %ret, align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %ret)
  ret i32 %8
}

declare hidden noundef nonnull align 4 dereferenceable(4) ptr @span_checked_access(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(16), i64 noundef)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr captures(none)) #0

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr captures(none)) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define dso_local void @_Z7computeRSt6vectorIiSaIiEEy(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %data, i64 noundef %numElems) {
entry:
  %data.addr = alloca ptr, align 8
  %numElems.addr = alloca i64, align 8
  %i = alloca i64, align 8
  %cleanup.dest.slot = alloca i32, align 4
  %j = alloca i64, align 8
  store ptr %data, ptr %data.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  store i64 %numElems, ptr %numElems.addr, align 8, !tbaa !4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %i)
  store i64 0, ptr %i, align 8, !tbaa !4
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.inc6, %entry
  %0 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %0, 100
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  store i32 2, ptr %cleanup.dest.slot, align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %i)
  br label %for.end8

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %j)
  store i64 0, ptr %j, align 8, !tbaa !4
  br label %for.cond1

for.cond1:                                        ; preds = %for.inc, %for.body
  %1 = load i64, ptr %j, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %2 = load i64, ptr %numElems.addr, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i64 %1, %2
  br i1 %cmp2, label %for.body4, label %for.cond.cleanup3

for.cond.cleanup3:                                ; preds = %for.cond1
  store i32 5, ptr %cleanup.dest.slot, align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %j)
  br label %for.end

for.body4:                                        ; preds = %for.cond1
  %3 = load ptr, ptr %data.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %4 = load i64, ptr %j, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %call = call noundef nonnull align 4 dereferenceable(4) ptr @_ZNSt6vectorIiSaIiEEixEm(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %3, i64 noundef %4)
  %5 = load i32, ptr %call, align 4, !tbaa !6
  %inc = add nsw i32 %5, 1
  store i32 %inc, ptr %call, align 4, !tbaa !6
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %for.body4
  %6 = load i64, ptr %j, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %inc5 = add i64 %6, 1
  store i64 %inc5, ptr %j, align 8, !tbaa !4
  br label %for.cond1, !llvm.loop !8

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup3
  br label %for.inc6

for.inc6:                                         ; preds = %for.end
  %7 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %inc7 = add i64 %7, 1
  store i64 %inc7, ptr %i, align 8, !tbaa !4
  br label %for.cond, !llvm.loop !10

for.end8:                                         ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup
  ret void
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr captures(none)) #0

declare dso_local noundef nonnull align 4 dereferenceable(4) ptr @_ZNSt6vectorIiSaIiEEixEm(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24), i64 noundef) align 2

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr captures(none)) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }

!0 = !{!1, !1, i64 0}
!1 = !{!"any pointer", !2, i64 0}
!2 = !{!"omnipotent char", !3, i64 0}
!3 = !{!"Simple C++ TBAA"}
!4 = !{!5, !5, i64 0}
!5 = !{!"long long", !2, i64 0}
!6 = !{!7, !7, i64 0}
!7 = !{!"int", !2, i64 0}
!8 = distinct !{!8, !9}
!9 = !{!"llvm.loop.mustprogress"}
!10 = distinct !{!10, !9}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr captures(none)) #0

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr captures(none)) #0

declare hidden noundef i64 @alloc(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24))

declare hidden noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(8) ptr @operator_acc(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24), i64 noundef)

define void @loop_with_signed_induction(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %vec) {
entry:
  %vec.addr = alloca ptr, align 8
  %count = alloca i64, align 8
  %i = alloca i64, align 8
  store ptr %vec, ptr %vec.addr, align 8
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %count)
  %0 = load ptr, ptr %vec.addr, align 8
  %call = call noundef i64 @alloc(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %0)
  store i64 %call, ptr %count, align 8
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %i)
  store i64 0, ptr %i, align 8
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %1 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %2 = load i64, ptr %count, align 8
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %1, %2
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %i)
  br label %for.end

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  %3 = load ptr, ptr %vec.addr, align 8
  %4 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %call1 = call noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(8) ptr @operator_acc(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %3, i64 noundef %4)
  %5 = load double, ptr %call1, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %add = fadd double %5, 1.000000e+00
  store double %add, ptr %call1, align 8, !tbaa !0
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  %6 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %inc = add nsw i64 %6, 1
  store i64 %inc, ptr %i, align 8
  br label %for.cond

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %count)
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }

!0 = !{!1, !1, i64 0}
!1 = !{!"double", !2, i64 0}
!2 = !{!"omnipotent char", !3, i64 0}
!3 = !{!"Simple C++ TBAA"}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr captures(none)) #0

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr captures(none)) #0

define void @foo(ptr noalias noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %vec) {
entry:
  %vec.addr = alloca ptr, align 8
  %count = alloca i64, align 8
  %i = alloca i64, align 8
  store ptr %vec, ptr %vec.addr, align 8
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %count)
  %0 = load ptr, ptr %vec.addr, align 8
  %call = call noundef i64 @alloc(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %0)
  store i64 %call, ptr %count, align 8
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %i)
  store i64 0, ptr %i, align 8
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %1 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %2 = load i64, ptr %count, align 8
  %cmp = icmp ne i64 %1, %2
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %i)
  br label %for.end

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  %3 = load ptr, ptr %vec.addr, align 8
  %4 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %call1 = call noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(8) ptr @operator_acc(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %3, i64 noundef %4)
  %5 = load double, ptr %call1, align 8
  %add = fadd double %5, 1.000000e+00
  store double %add, ptr %call1, align 8
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  %6 = load i64, ptr %i, align 8
  %inc = add i64 %6, 1
  store i64 %inc, ptr %i, align 8
  br label %for.cond

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %count)
  ret void
}

declare hidden noundef i64 @alloc(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24))

declare hidden noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(8) ptr @operator_acc(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24), i64 noundef)

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }

comments.author: philnik777
comments.body: I'm not entirely sure this is a good idea, but I thought I'd let others have a look and decide whether this is worth pursuing.
